phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: This cluster combines unrelated conditions: sleep disorders and narcolepsy, spinal deformities (scoliosis/kyphosis), gynecologic neoplasia and cervical dysplasia, and ulcerative colitis. There is no unifying clinical pathway; it reflects disparate patient groups merged into one slot.

phenotypes.label: Sensorineural hearing loss and tinnitus
phenotypes.description: Cluster of otologic conditions centered on bilateral sensorineural hearing loss and tinnitus, with mixed or conductive hearing loss variants. Frequently accompanied by cerumen impaction, eustachian tube dysfunction, otitis media/externa, otalgia, dizziness/vertigo, and in some cases tympanic membrane perforation, otorrhea, or cholesteatoma. This represents chronic audiovestibular disease managed in ENT settings.

phenotypes.label: Lumbar spondylosis and radiculopathy
phenotypes.description: Degenerative lumbar spine disease with nerve root involvement dominates, presenting as chronic low back pain, sciatica, and neurogenic claudication. Features include lumbar spondylosis, disc degeneration/herniation, spinal stenosis, radiculopathy, spondylolisthesis, and post‑laminectomy syndrome, with co-occurring osteoarthritis and common comorbidities such as hypertension and GERD.

phenotypes.label: Diabetic and venous leg ulcers
phenotypes.description: Chronic lower-extremity wound disease dominated by diabetic foot and venous stasis ulcers with associated cellulitis and soft-tissue infection. Frequent complications include osteomyelitis, gangrene, lymphedema/edema, and tissue loss with toe or limb amputation and stump complications.

phenotypes.label: Rotator cuff and shoulder disorders
phenotypes.description: Cluster of shoulder musculoskeletal pathology including rotator cuff tears/strains, impingement, adhesive capsulitis, biceps tendinitis, labral injury/instability, dislocation, and glenohumeral osteoarthritis. Patients typically present with shoulder pain, stiffness, and functional limitation, often related to overuse or acute injury.

phenotypes.label: Substance use, mood and dental disease
phenotypes.description: Behavioral health conditions—major depression, PTSD, anxiety, and bipolar spectrum—co-occur with substance use disorders including alcohol, tobacco, opioids, cocaine, and polysubstance patterns. Prominent dental disease (caries, pulpitis, periodontal disease, tooth loss) is also clustered, consistent with morbidity seen in chronic substance use. Common primary-care comorbidities such as hypertension, hyperlipidemia, GERD, obesity, and back pain appear secondarily.

phenotypes.label: Mixed dermatologic, cardiac, stone disorders
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions span unrelated systems: non‑scarring and male‑pattern alopecia with panniculitis; left heart failure and valvular disease; and nephrolithiasis with hypercalciuria/hyperoxaluria. There is no unifying clinical mechanism, so this blends dermatologic, cardiac, and kidney stone patterns.

phenotypes.label: Post-thyroidectomy hypothyroidism and hypocalcemia
phenotypes.description: Thyroid carcinoma patients status post thyroidectomy with postoperative hypothyroidism and postsurgical hypoparathyroidism causing hypocalcemia. Related endocrine/electrolyte issues appear, including occasional hypercalcemia and vitamin D deficiency, with rare arrhythmic features (e.g., long QT) consistent with calcium disturbances.

phenotypes.label: Vitreoretinal disease and diabetic retinopathy
phenotypes.description: Ophthalmic cluster centered on retinal detachment, vitreous hemorrhage, and diabetic retinopathy with macular edema, often accompanied by visual impairment and blindness. Refractive errors, glaucoma, cataract, retinal vascular occlusions, and scotoma further indicate advanced vitreoretinal pathology and complications of proliferative diabetic eye disease.

phenotypes.label: Hypertensive retinopathy with macular edema
phenotypes.description: Patients show hypertensive retinopathy with cystoid macular changes and branch retinal vein occlusion, often with macular edema. Associated findings include glaucoma and unilateral blindness, indicating advanced retinal vascular disease with significant vision loss risk.

phenotypes.label: Parkinsonism, tremor, and cognitive decline
phenotypes.description: Neurologic phenotype featuring parkinsonism and tremor (including REM sleep behavior disorder, bradykinesia, and abnormal involuntary movements) alongside cognitive impairment and dementias. Vitamin B12 deficiency, pernicious anemia, and megaloblastic anemia are prominent, reflecting a blend of neurodegenerative and deficiency-related neurocognitive presentations.

phenotypes.label: Benign prostatic hyperplasia with urinary symptoms
phenotypes.description: Male genitourinary disorders centered on benign prostatic hyperplasia with lower urinary tract symptoms (frequency, urgency, nocturia, poor stream), urinary retention, and obstruction. Associated conditions include prostatitis, raised PSA, hematuria, urinary tract infection, epididymitis, testicular pain/hypofunction, erectile dysfunction, and occasional kidney stones or prostate cancer.

phenotypes.label: Giant cell arteritis and PMR
phenotypes.description: Conditions center on polymyalgia rheumatica with temporal (giant cell) arteritis, arteritis, jaw claudication, arterial aneurysm, and ocular ischemic events, consistent with large‑vessel vasculitis in older adults. Related vasculitides and inflammatory arthritis co-occur; pelvic floor disorders appear in some records but are incidental to the vasculitis pattern.

phenotypes.label: Type 2 diabetes with microvascular complications
phenotypes.description: Patients with type 2 diabetes, often with poor glycemic control, clustered with hypertension, hyperlipidemia, and obesity. There is prominent involvement of microvascular complications including diabetic neuropathy, retinopathy, and diabetic kidney disease.

phenotypes.label: Chronic pain with depression
phenotypes.description: Dominated by chronic pain syndrome and central pain, with recurrent major depression and PTSD as frequent psychiatric comorbidities. Trauma-related sequelae such as limb amputation also appear, consistent with complex chronic pain with substantial psychosocial burden.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions combine cranial and facial nerve disorders and orofacial pain (Bell’s palsy, trigeminal neuralgia, hemifacial spasm, acoustic neuroma) with separate clusters of burn/trauma diagnoses and behavioral crises (overdose, suicidal behavior/intent). These represent unrelated neurologic, injury, and psychiatric conditions rather than a single coherent clinical pattern.

phenotypes.label: Cerebellar/vestibular ataxia and imbalance
phenotypes.description: Cluster centers on balance and coordination disorders with vestibular and cerebellar involvement, featuring ataxia, nystagmus, dizziness, and gait instability. Structural and neurologic contributors appear, including hydrocephalus, intracerebral hemorrhage, hereditary motor–sensory neuropathy, and vitamin deficiency, with mobility impairment common and occasional chronic hypoxemic respiratory failure or complex regional pain syndrome.

phenotypes.label: Venous thromboembolism and thrombophilia
phenotypes.description: Cluster of venous thromboembolism centered on lower‑extremity thrombosis with embolism, alongside generic venous/arterial thromboembolism. Inherited and acquired thrombophilias (lupus anticoagulant, prothrombin G20210A) and sickle cell trait appear, with complications such as splenic infarction and conjunctival hemorrhage, consistent with a hypercoagulable state.

phenotypes.label: Plasma cell dyscrasias with complications
phenotypes.description: Plasma cell disorders dominate, including multiple myeloma, monoclonal gammopathy, and amyloidosis. Co-occurring features include hypogammaglobulinemia and leukopenia, venous thromboembolism, vertebral compression fractures, and organ involvement such as nephrotic syndrome and amyloid cardiomyopathy; some patients are noted in remission.

phenotypes.label: Unused low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions are generic comorbidities and nonspecific symptoms (e.g., hyperlipidemia, hypertension, chest pain, nausea) with no unifying clinical theme. This does not form a usable condition cluster for cohort definition.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions split between chronic lymphoid leukemia/lymphocytosis with herpesviral mucocutaneous infections and a separate cluster of degenerative shoulder disorders and benign lipomas. These groups do not share a single unifying clinical mechanism, so this does not represent one coherent condition pattern.

phenotypes.label: End-stage kidney disease and transplant
phenotypes.description: Advanced chronic kidney disease, including ESRD, clustering with dialysis- and kidney transplant–related conditions. Typical complications include anemia of CKD, secondary hyperparathyroidism/renal osteodystrophy, hyperkalemia, proteinuria, hematuria, and hypertensive renal failure. Etiologies and contributors include hypertensive nephrosclerosis and cystic kidney disease, with immune dysfunction noted in transplant contexts.

phenotypes.label: Alcoholic cirrhosis with portal hypertension
phenotypes.description: Alcohol-related cirrhosis with portal hypertension and decompensation, featuring ascites, varices with gastrointestinal bleeding, hepatic encephalopathy, and hepatic failure. Associated findings include thrombocytopenia/pancytopenia with splenomegaly, portal vein thrombosis, spontaneous bacterial peritonitis, gallstone and bile duct disease, and acute/chronic pancreatitis.

phenotypes.label: Seasonal allergic rhinitis with conjunctivitis
phenotypes.description: Patients show seasonal/perennial allergic rhinitis tied to pollen, animal dander, or house dust mite, frequently accompanied by allergic conjunctivitis. Urticaria and angioedema are present, with occasional mild intermittent asthma, reflecting an atopic diathesis across upper airway and ocular surfaces.

phenotypes.label: Chronic comorbidity catch-all
phenotypes.description: General primary‑care comorbidity cluster with GERD, hypothyroidism, hypertension, anxiety/depression, fatigue, obesity, migraines/fibromyalgia, vitamin D deficiency, iron‑deficiency anemia, and obstructive sleep apnea. It also includes common nonspecific complaints such as chest pain, dyspnea, palpitations, and dizziness, reflecting broad baseline burden rather than a single disease phenotype. Some women’s health conditions and irritable bowel symptoms appear but within a general medical context.

phenotypes.label: Chronic comorbidity catch-all
phenotypes.description: Common adult comorbidities and symptoms: hypertension, hyperlipidemia, obesity, type 2 diabetes, GERD, obstructive sleep apnea, and chronic musculoskeletal pain. Mental health conditions such as PTSD, anxiety, depression, and insomnia frequently co-occur. This reflects a broad primary‑care baseline rather than a single disease phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Sleep-disordered breathing and pulmonary hypertension
phenotypes.description: Sleep-disordered breathing (obstructive and central sleep apnea and hypoventilation) co-occurs with hypoxemia, dyspnea, and morbid obesity. Pulmonary vascular complications are prominent, including pulmonary hypertension, cor pulmonale, right ventricular strain, and pulmonary embolism (sometimes with infarction), with connective tissue disease as a related etiology. This reflects patients with sleep-related breathing disorders complicated by pulmonary hypertension and thromboembolic disease.

phenotypes.label: High-risk pregnancy and obstetric complications
phenotypes.description: Conditions center on pregnancy across trimesters with documentation of gestational age and antenatal findings. It emphasizes high‑risk pregnancy and obstetric complications, including pre‑existing diabetes or hypertension in pregnancy, placental disorders, malpresentation (breech), polyhydramnios, antepartum hemorrhage, and false labor. Maternal factors such as obesity, anemia, RhD negativity, infertility history, and prior uterine scar are also represented.

phenotypes.label: Aortic and mitral valve disease
phenotypes.description: Pattern centers on valvular heart disease, with aortic sclerosis/regurgitation and mitral stenosis or regurgitation, including rheumatic valve disease. Associated findings include atrial fibrillation, cardiomegaly, endocarditis, and presence of a prosthetic heart valve, reflecting advanced native or prosthetic valve pathology.

phenotypes.label: Wrist and hand injuries and disorders
phenotypes.description: Cluster of wrist and hand conditions including joint pain, osteoarthritis of the hand and wrist (thumb CMC), carpal tunnel syndrome, De Quervain tenosynovitis, and ganglion cysts. Frequent associated acute injuries include distal radius and carpal fractures and wrist sprains with joint effusion and stiffness. This reflects patients with both acute trauma and chronic degenerative or overuse pathology of the distal upper extremity.

phenotypes.label: Ear infection (otitis)
phenotypes.description: This cluster is dominated by ear infection diagnoses, with related findings such as otalgia and otorrhea, consistent with acute otitis media or externa presentations. Other conditions appear as low-weight background comorbidities and do not define the pattern.

phenotypes.label: Osteoporotic and traumatic fractures
phenotypes.description: Orthopedic trauma and fragility fractures, centered on proximal femur (neck/intertrochanteric) and extending to clavicle, ribs, pelvis, and femoral shaft. Osteoporosis and osteoporotic fractures are prominent, with healing complications such as nonunion, delayed union, and sequelae like acquired limb-length discrepancy. This reflects patients with significant fractures and related musculoskeletal pain/injury codes.

phenotypes.label: Systemic lupus with renal involvement
phenotypes.description: Autoimmune connective tissue disease centered on systemic lupus erythematosus with renal involvement (proteinuria, glomerulonephritis, early CKD). Co-occurring features include mucocutaneous disease (discoid lesions, photosensitivity, oral ulcers, alopecia), serositis/pericardial disease, Raynaud’s with systemic sclerosis/Sjögren’s overlap, high autoantibody titers, and antiphospholipid syndrome.

phenotypes.label: Colorectal cancer with metastases and stoma
phenotypes.description: Cluster centers on colorectal and other gastrointestinal malignancies with metastatic spread to liver, lung, bone, and peritoneum. It includes postoperative/ostomy-related intestinal complications (ileostomy/colostomy, parastomal hernia, fistula), cancer pain and fatigue, chemotherapy-associated neutropenia and anemia, and GI symptom flares with dehydration, electrolyte disturbances, acute kidney injury, sepsis, abdominal pain, and nausea/vomiting.

phenotypes.label: Eyelash malposition with corneal disease
phenotypes.description: Cluster of ocular surface pathology featuring eyelash malposition (trichiasis/distichiasis) with corneal opacity and degeneration. Associated findings include tear film insufficiency, blepharitis, ptosis, and vitreous opacities, consistent with chronic eyelid–corneal irritation and corneal surface disease.

phenotypes.label: Unused low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions are generic chronic comorbidities and common symptoms without a unifying clinical pattern. The rarer items are scattered and unrelated, each contributing negligibly. This does not represent a coherent clinical phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Ocular surface disease with sarcoidosis
phenotypes.description: Cluster of ophthalmic conditions centered on dry eye (tear film insufficiency, keratoconjunctivitis sicca), blepharitis and meibomian gland dysfunction, with associated eyelid disorders and corneal disease (keratitis, corneal edema, corneal transplant status). Sarcoidosis (including pulmonary) frequently co-occurs, pointing to inflammatory/autoimmune ocular involvement; some patients also have high myopia, retinal detachment, and pseudophakia/anterior chamber lens status.

phenotypes.label: Stroke and post-stroke sequelae
phenotypes.description: Pattern centers on acute cerebrovascular events—ischemic cerebral infarction (including embolic MCA strokes), intracranial hemorrhage, aneurysm—and transient ischemic attack. Prominent late effects include hemiplegia/hemiparesis, aphasia, dysarthria, incoordination, facial weakness, and cognitive deficits, reflecting post‑stroke sequelae tied to cerebrovascular disease and atherosclerosis. Hypertension and hyperlipidemia appear as accompanying vascular risk conditions.

phenotypes.label: Peripheral polyneuropathies and mononeuropathies
phenotypes.description: Peripheral nerve disorders dominate, including polyneuropathy, mononeuropathy/mononeuritis, and demyelinating forms such as chronic inflammatory demyelinating polyradiculoneuropathy, with features like altered sensation, foot drop, impaired balance, myoclonus, and ocular motor abnormalities. Etiologies represented include idiopathic, hereditary, inflammatory/toxic, and neuropathies associated with systemic disease such as Waldenström macroglobulinemia.

phenotypes.label: Knee osteoarthritis and meniscal injury
phenotypes.description: Degenerative knee joint disease centered on osteoarthritis, with bilateral knee pain, effusion, and arthropathy. Meniscal tears or derangements and ligament sprains commonly contribute to chronic knee symptoms, with obesity and hypertension frequently co-occurring.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions mix common chronic diagnoses such as hypertension, hyperlipidemia, diabetes, and GERD with scattered unrelated problems. There is no coherent clinical theme to define a patient group.

phenotypes.label: Concussion and postconcussion sequelae
phenotypes.description: Cluster centers on mild traumatic brain injury with persistent symptoms: concussion and head injury diagnoses accompanied by postconcussion syndrome, post-traumatic headache, amnesia, and sleep disturbance. Late effects of intracranial injury and focal issues such as speech disturbance indicate ongoing neurologic sequelae after head trauma.

phenotypes.label: Venous thromboembolism and thrombophilia
phenotypes.description: Cluster centers on venous thromboembolism (DVT/PE) with hypercoagulable states, including hereditary thrombophilia (Factor V Leiden), essential thrombocythemia, and hyperhomocysteinemia. Thyroid disease—particularly thyrotoxicosis with post‑ablative hypothyroidism—and bladder malignancy suggest secondary prothrombotic contributors. Valvular findings and murmurs appear incidentally alongside the core thrombosis profile.

phenotypes.label: Bipolar spectrum and congenital heart disease
phenotypes.description: Leading conditions split between bipolar spectrum disorders (multiple bipolar subtypes, schizoaffective disorder, obsessive–compulsive disorder) and congenital/aortopathic heart disease with ventricular tachyarrhythmias and valve abnormalities. Endocrine entries such as diabetes insipidus and drug‑induced hypothyroidism also appear. These represent unrelated clinical themes without a single unifying mechanism.

phenotypes.label: HIV with STIs and HPV dysplasia
phenotypes.description: HIV-positive patients, often coded as asymptomatic infection, clustered with multiple sexually transmitted infections. HPV-related anogenital and cervical dysplasia, including carcinoma in situ, are prominent. Lipodystrophy and viral hepatitis co-infections are also represented.

phenotypes.label: Mixed ocular, enthesopathy, and anemia
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions span ocular-retinal disease (central serous chorioretinopathy and serous RPE detachment), ankle/foot enthesopathy with Achilles bursitis and heel pain, and deficiency anemias. Additional outliers include endometrial hyperplasia, cholesterolosis of the gallbladder, and anal fistula. There is no single clinical theme across these conditions.

phenotypes.label: CMV enteritis in immunocompromised
phenotypes.description: Opportunistic cytomegalovirus–associated gastrointestinal infection and viral enteritis in immunocompromised patients, including those following bone marrow transplant. Presentations include diarrhea and febrile illness with pancytopenia and hypogammaglobulinemia. Neurologic involvement such as myelitis and post‑herpetic neuritis may occur, reflecting profound immune dysfunction.

phenotypes.label: Epilepsy and seizure disorders
phenotypes.description: Epilepsy with recurrent seizures is central, spanning focal (localization-related, complex and simple partial) and generalized epilepsies, with some cases of status epilepticus. Headache syndromes and intracranial pressure findings (benign intracranial hypertension with optic disc edema) and altered mental status appear as related neurologic presentations. Conversion disorder is occasionally coded as a seizure mimic in this population.

phenotypes.label: Age-related ocular disorders cluster
phenotypes.description: Patients with age-related eye disease, including cataracts, refractive errors (presbyopia, myopia, hyperopia, astigmatism), dry eye, vitreous degeneration/floaters, epiretinal membrane, and retinal drusen. The pattern also includes the glaucoma spectrum (primary open-angle, ocular hypertension, low-tension) and eyelid dermatochalasis, consistent with older adults carrying multiple chronic ophthalmic diagnoses.

phenotypes.label: Gout and hyperuricemia with tophi
phenotypes.description: Cluster centers on gout and urate deposition disorders, including chronic gout with or without tophi, gouty arthropathy, and hyperuricemia. Comorbid chronic kidney disease appears in some patients, consistent with urate burden and shared metabolic risks.

phenotypes.label: Obstructive uropathy with pyelonephritis
phenotypes.description: Cluster centered on hydronephrosis and ureteral obstruction (stones, ureteral stricture/occlusion) with associated upper urinary tract infection including pyelonephritis and pyonephrosis. Tubulointerstitial nephritis and Escherichia coli urinary infection are frequent co-occurrences, reflecting obstructive uropathy complicated by infection.

phenotypes.label: Anxiety and depression with migraine
phenotypes.description: Patients with anxiety and mood disorders (generalized anxiety disorder, recurrent major depression, panic disorder) who also have migraine (including chronic/intractable), headaches, insomnia, and chronic musculoskeletal pain (low back pain, myalgia, sciatica). The pattern includes somatic symptom presentations and occasional attentional symptoms.

phenotypes.label: Rheumatoid arthritis spectrum
phenotypes.description: Autoimmune inflammatory arthritis centered on rheumatoid arthritis (seropositive and seronegative), with polyarticular involvement and synovitis. Related rheumatic conditions are present (ankylosing spondylitis, Sjögren’s), along with bone fragility diagnoses such as osteoporosis and vitamin D deficiency. Degenerative osteoarthritis and fibromyalgia co-occur, reflecting chronic joint disease burden.

phenotypes.label: Type 1 diabetes with complications
phenotypes.description: Autoimmune type 1 diabetes with dysglycemia (hyperglycemia, hypoglycemia, DKA) and classic microvascular complications including retinopathy, neuropathy, nephropathy, and gastroparesis. Frequent co-occurring autoimmune conditions (Hashimoto thyroiditis, celiac disease) reinforce an autoimmune clustering pattern; malignancy codes are present but not central.

phenotypes.label: Structural lung disease with transplantation
phenotypes.description: Cluster of advanced structural lung disease, including bronchiectasis, cystic fibrosis, interstitial lung disease, and pulmonary fibrosis, with dyspnea and cough. Many patients show lung (and some heart) transplant status with transplant-related complications, alongside colonization/infection by nontuberculous mycobacteria, aspergillosis, and MRSA. Cystic fibrosis–associated exocrine pancreatic insufficiency and secondary diabetes also appear.

phenotypes.label: Intracranial neoplasms and meningitis
phenotypes.description: Central nervous system pathology centered on intracranial disease. Dominant signals are brain neoplasms (primary and benign) with abnormal brain imaging, cerebral edema, focal visual field loss, and headaches. Meningitis (including viral) and meningeal disorders co-occur, with melanoma suggesting possible brain involvement and postoperative states common in this group.

phenotypes.label: Female breast cancer and sequelae
phenotypes.description: Female breast malignancy and in situ disease dominate, including quadrant-specific primary tumors, ER status, and nodal involvement. Screening and diagnostic breast findings (lump, abnormal mammogram, microcalcifications) appear alongside postsurgical states such as mastectomy, reconstructed-breast deformity, and postmastectomy lymphedema. Menopausal status and bone disorders/osteoporosis are frequent comorbid conditions in this group.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: Top conditions split between an asthma/airways cluster (asthma of varying severity, acute exacerbations, cough, dyspnea, upper respiratory infections, allergic rhinitis) and neurobehavioral/psychiatric diagnoses (ADHD subtypes, bipolar spectrum, PTSD, childhood emotional/developmental disorders). These represent unrelated organ systems without a unifying clinical mechanism, so this appears to be an amalgam rather than a single phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Chronic liver disease and cirrhosis
phenotypes.description: Chronic liver disease across multiple etiologies, including hepatitis C, nonalcoholic fatty liver disease/NASH, autoimmune hepatitis, and primary biliary cholangitis. Many patients exhibit hepatic fibrosis and cirrhosis with abnormal liver enzymes, and some have hepatocellular carcinoma or right upper quadrant pain consistent with advanced liver involvement.

phenotypes.label: Chronic rhinosinusitis and nasal obstruction
phenotypes.description: Cluster of sinonasal disorders centered on chronic rhinosinusitis with variants (maxillary, ethmoid, frontal, pansinusitis) and recurrent acute flares. Co-occurring nasal obstruction and congestion are linked to rhinitis (allergic, vasomotor) and structural factors such as septal deviation, turbinate hypertrophy, and nasal valve incompetence. Nasal polyps, postnasal drip, epistaxis, and reduced smell further characterize an ENT-focused airway phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: Two unrelated clusters dominate: upper-extremity musculoskeletal disorders (lateral/medial epicondylitis, elbow pain, bursal/cartilage disorders) and thoracic aortic disease (thoracic aortic aneurysm, aortic dissection) with scattered vascular/neurologic items. The leading conditions do not form a single clinical story, so this is not a usable single phenotype.

phenotypes.label: COPD, lung nodules, and lung cancer
phenotypes.description: Respiratory disease centered on COPD/emphysema with abnormal chest imaging (solitary pulmonary nodule, atelectasis, pleural effusion) and symptoms such as dyspnea and cough. Primary lung malignancy is frequent, with upper/lower lobe cancers and related entities; occasional lymphoma and neuroendocrine tumor codes appear within this thoracic oncology spectrum.

phenotypes.label: Hypersomnia, goiter, and hand disorders
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions combine sleep complaints (hypersomnia, snoring, insomnia), benign thyroid nodules/goiter, and hand tendon disorders (trigger finger, ganglion), with additional scattered dermatologic and infectious conditions (vitiligo, pulmonary tuberculosis). These are unrelated clinical themes rather than a single condition, so this pattern is not a cohesive phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Photoaging, keratoses, and skin cancers
phenotypes.description: Sun-exposed skin conditions predominate: actinic keratoses, solar lentigines/photoaging changes, and rosacea, with many benign lesions such as seborrheic keratoses, melanocytic nevi, hemangiomas, and cherry angiomas. Nonmelanoma skin cancers (basal and squamous cell carcinomas) are present, suggesting dermatology visits for chronic photodamage and cutaneous neoplasms, often in older adults.

phenotypes.label: Acute symptom catch-all
phenotypes.description: Common acute complaints—pain, nausea/vomiting, shortness of breath, chest and abdominal pain—cluster with anxiety and gastrointestinal symptoms. Frequently accompanied by prevalent chronic comorbidities such as hypertension, GERD, asthma, obesity, and diabetes. This reflects a general presenting-symptom baseline rather than a single disease pattern.

phenotypes.label: Acute cardiopulmonary and gastrointestinal symptoms
phenotypes.description: Presentation dominated by acute symptoms such as chest pain, shortness of breath, wheezing, rales, orthopnea, and cyanosis, together with gastrointestinal complaints including nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, constipation, and abdominal pain. Also includes distress, anxiety, syncope, and bleeding, consistent with emergency or urgent evaluations rather than a single specific disease.

phenotypes.label: Spinal somatic dysfunction and pain
phenotypes.description: Cluster of regional somatic dysfunctions of the spine and pelvis (cervical, thoracic, lumbar, sacral, pelvic) with associated axial pain, neck pain, and back muscle strain/spasm. Reflects patients presenting with musculoskeletal and postural disorders of the trunk and rib cage, often coded across multiple contiguous regions.

phenotypes.label: Head and neck cancer with dysphagia
phenotypes.description: Head and neck malignancies centered on the oral cavity, oropharynx, tongue, and tonsil with associated neck masses, dysphagia, dysphonia, and throat pain. Salivary gland disorders (sialadenitis, sialolithiasis, xerostomia) and oral mucosal lesions co-occur, reflecting an ENT head‑and‑neck oncology presentation.

phenotypes.label: Neurogenic bladder with SCI complications
phenotypes.description: Neurogenic bladder and bowel in patients with spinal cord disorders (paraplegia/tetraplegia, spina bifida), showing frequent urinary tract infections, urinary retention, and catheter-related infections. Pressure ulcers of the sacrum/buttocks and wheelchair dependence reflect immobility-related complications. Overall, this captures neurogenic lower urinary tract dysfunction with associated skin breakdown and infectious complications.

phenotypes.label: Ankle-foot fractures and psoriatic arthropathy
phenotypes.description: Conditions center on ankle and foot pathology: fractures of tibia/fibula, malleoli, calcaneus and metatarsals, sprains, and focal pain with arthralgia. Psoriasis and psoriatic arthritis are prominent, indicating inflammatory involvement of these joints alongside osteoarthritis, flatfoot, tendinopathy, and complications or infections related to internal orthopedic devices.

phenotypes.label: Common comorbidities with pain and GERD
phenotypes.description: General adult patients with common chronic conditions such as hypertension, hyperlipidemia, hypothyroidism, obesity, and GERD, often accompanied by anxiety/depression. Musculoskeletal pain syndromes are prominent (low back pain, joint pain, osteoarthritis, disc disease), with additional respiratory and gastrointestinal complaints including asthma, abdominal pain, peptic disease, diverticulitis, and hemorrhoids.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: Leading conditions split across unrelated systems: multiple sclerosis with optic neuritis and other CNS demyelination, Crohn’s disease with segment-specific involvement, inner ear disorders (Ménière’s, sudden hearing loss), and dermatologic diagnoses (acne, alopecia). There is no single clinical pathway tying these together, so this grouping is not a cohesive phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Age-related macular degeneration
phenotypes.description: Dominant signal is age-related macular degeneration (nonexudative and exudative) with associated retinal pathology, including macular/retinal degeneration, retinal neovascularization, central retinal vein occlusion, vitreoretinal adhesion, and vitreous changes; cataract appears as well. Co-occurring diagnoses include tuberculin test reactions or inactive tuberculosis, abdominal wall hernias, and renal cysts, reflecting age-related comorbidity in this population.

phenotypes.label: Cervical dystonia and neck pain
phenotypes.description: Cluster of neuromuscular neck disorders centered on cervical dystonia/torticollis alongside degenerative cervical spine disease and prominent neck pain. Presentations include painful neck spasm, limited range of motion, and cervicogenic headaches, with occasional dizziness/vertigo noted.

phenotypes.label: Herpes zoster and EDS/dysautonomia
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions split between herpes zoster (including ophthalmic involvement and postherpetic neuralgia) and an autonomic/connective‑tissue cluster. The latter includes Ehlers‑Danlos syndrome, postural orthostatic tachycardia syndrome, familial dysautonomia, ocular anomalies (amblyopia, strabismus, keratoconus), and congenital heart disease. These represent unrelated clinical mechanisms, indicating a merged set of themes rather than a single phenotype.

phenotypes.label: CAD with AF and heart failure
phenotypes.description: Atherosclerotic coronary artery disease and ischemic heart disease with atrial fibrillation/flutter, conduction disease, and heart failure (including hypertensive and diastolic forms). Many have prior myocardial infarction and post-coronary bypass graft status, with dyspnea or angina, and comorbid hypertension, hyperlipidemia, chronic kidney disease, COPD, and obstructive sleep apnea.

phenotypes.label: Peripheral arterial disease and claudication
phenotypes.description: Atherosclerotic peripheral arterial disease of the lower limbs, with intermittent claudication, rest pain, and arterial stenosis/occlusion. Carotid artery disease and transient cerebrovascular insufficiency appear as part of systemic atherosclerosis. Hypertension and hyperlipidemia commonly co-occur as vascular risk factors.

phenotypes.label: Vaginitis and genital infections
phenotypes.description: Cluster of vulvovaginal conditions, dominated by vaginitis with Candida and trichomonas, and occasional gonorrhea, presenting with leukorrhea and genital pruritus. Cervical cytology with low‑grade squamous intraepithelial lesion appears, indicating HPV‑associated cervical dysplasia within this group. Hidradenitis and localized skin abscesses in intertriginous/genital areas are also represented, reflecting overlap with recurrent skin infection/inflammation.

phenotypes.label: Cardiopulmonary decompensation with effusions
phenotypes.description: Acute cardiopulmonary presentations with pleural and pericardial effusions, pneumothorax, dyspnea, and chest pain, with complications such as pulmonary embolism and hypercapnia. Many have underlying heart failure and valvular disease with peripheral edema and syncope, often accompanied by kidney dysfunction (acute renal failure, uremia, hyperphosphatemia), reflecting a cardiorenal picture.

phenotypes.label: Substance use and induced psychiatric syndromes
phenotypes.description: Pattern of substance use disorders (alcohol, opioids, stimulants, cocaine, cannabis) with intoxication, withdrawal, and substance‑induced mood or psychotic episodes. Often accompanied by schizophrenia/schizoaffective or bipolar disorder, suicidality, and nicotine dependence, with presentations including overdose and acute psychosis.
